summary: Seat-invite / Monthly Cooperative team-management feature verified end-to-end. Backend: 16/16 new pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_seat_invites.py all pass, covering GET /api/account/seats (monthly + free), POST /api/account/invite (owner happy path, non-owner 403, free 400 with 'Monthly Cooperative' copy, team-full 400), GET /api/account/invite/{token} (valid/expired/used/404, public — no auth required), POST /api/account/invite/accept (account_id swap verified in DB and via /auth/me, replay returns 400 'already been used', joinee's /usage/me flips to tier='monthly' with monthly_prompts_cap=250, monthly_usage row keyed by account_id confirms pool sharing), DELETE /api/account/seats/{user_id} (teammate moves to a NEW free personal account; self-removal blocked with 400 'cannot remove themselves'), and DELETE /api/account/invite/{token} for revoke. Full suite is 43/43 green (16 new + 27 prior regression). Frontend (mobile 390x844, real EXPO_BACKEND_URL, sessions injected into localStorage as auth_token): (1) monthly-owner Account screen shows 'Manage your team' testID='manage-team-btn'; (2) free-tier Account screen correctly HIDES manage-team-btn; (3) /account/seats renders '1 OF 3 SEATS USED', owner seat row with '· Owner · You', invite-label-input and invite-create-btn — clicking creates an invite and the result block exposes the join URL of shape '<base>/account/join?token=<12-hex>'; (4) /account/join?token=<valid> with a signed-in user shows testID='join-title' ('Join UI Owner on Code Without Limits?'), italic label '“Maria”', and testID='join-accept-btn'; signed-out user instead sees testID='join-signin-btn' that routes to /welcome with the join URL preserved. No deprecated Expo packages introduced in the new code (no expo-av/expo-barcode-scanner/expo-background-fetch).

minor.issue: seats list is capped to 10 via .to_list(10) and pending invites to 10. With seats_allowed=3 this is fine, but if seats_allowed ever increases the cap will silently truncate. /app/backend/server.py:604,609.

Race-window note in the docstring claims the seat cap re-check is transactional, but Motor does not start a real MongoDB transaction here — two simultaneous accepts on the same single open seat could theoretically both pass count_documents and create a 4th seat. Low-risk for a 3-seat plan, but worth noting. /app/backend/server.py:712-715.

Acceptance deletes the joinee's orphaned personal account but does NOT clean up monthly_usage / daily_usage / payments rows scoped to the old account_id. Likely fine (the joinee was on free), but mention if a paying user (day_pass) accepts a monthly invite — their previous account row is dropped while related usage stays orphaned.

issues: Gate copy says 'invite up to 2 teammates' which is consistent with 3-seat plan (1 owner + 2). OK.

After invite is created, the 'invite-result' block appears below the fold on a 390x844 viewport when the seats list is at the top of the scroll view; users might miss it. Consider scrolling to the result on success or moving the result block above the form.

'Manage your team' link uses Ionicons + Text but lacks visual emphasis (no card background) compared to the surrounding statsCard/tierCard. Functional, but easy to overlook between the stats card and the tier list.

react_native_issues.issue: Pre-existing console warning 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow.' still present (not introduced by this iteration). Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/account.tsx:262-269 (tierCardActive).
Pre-existing console warning 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents.' carried over from previous iterations.

action_items: Optional polish: auto-scroll to (or move above the form) the 'invite-result' block in /app/frontend/app/account/seats.tsx so newly created invite link is immediately visible on small screens.
Optional: replace .to_list(10) with .to_list(None) (or seats_allowed) in /app/backend/server.py:604,609 so the listing never silently truncates if seats_allowed changes later.
Optional doc: address the soft race window in POST /api/account/invite/accept (server.py:712-715) — either with a Mongo transaction or a $inc-and-check atomic update.
Carry-over: address pre-existing React Native Web deprecation warnings (shadow* → boxShadow, pointerEvents prop → style.pointerEvents). Not introduced by this iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: All new endpoints correctly use the /api prefix via the apiRouter (server.py:597,625,666,694,738,750) — good.

POST /api/account/invite uses uuid.uuid4().hex[:12] (12 hex chars ≈ 48 bits). Acceptable for a 14-day single-use invite shared via private channels, but consider secrets.token_urlsafe(16) for stronger entropy.
GET /api/account/invite/{token} is correctly unauthenticated (matches the review request) and only returns owner_name + label — no account_id / created_by leak. Good.
DELETE /api/account/seats/{seat_user_id} creates a new account with seats_allowed=1, tier='free', is_account_owner=True for the removed teammate (server.py:765-777). Matches spec.
GET /api/account/seats does not require ownership — every seat on the account can read it. This matches the docstring 'Anyone on the account can read this so they know who else is on the team.'
FRONTEND_URL is read from /app/backend/.env and used to build join_url — verified the produced URL uses the public preview origin.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests use the existing seed_user fixture in conftest.py (already cleans up after each test). For UI testing I temporarily seeded two users in Mongo with predictable session tokens (sess_UI_owner_token monthly+owner, sess_UI_free_token free) plus one invite token 'uitoken123abc'; all seeded rows were removed at the end of the run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Seat-invite endpoints live under /api/account/* in server.py:597-778. New collection account_invites is indexed on token (unique) and account_id. The fixture pattern in conftest.py auto-cleans seeded users/accounts/sessions but does NOT clean account_invites or monthly_usage rows the seat tests touch — each test class explicitly deletes its own account_invites docs. For UI sessions you can write straight into Mongo and stash the session_token into web localStorage under key 'auth_token' (TOKEN_KEY in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.ts:14). When testing the join screen as a signed-in user, set auth_token AFTER calling page.goto('/welcome') so the WebStorage origin matches.
